paragraphs.text: Pinterest
24 October 2025

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act

paragraphs.text: Pinterest submits the below response to the European Commission (the "Commission")'s Call for Evidence opened 17 July 2025, on the Digital Fairness Act ("DFA"). Pinterest is pleased to have the opportunity to engage with the European Commission on this topic and we appreciate the Commission's efforts to gather feedback prior to further developing the DFA initiative.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Introduction to Pinterest

paragraphs.text: Pinterest is a visual search and discovery platform where users around the world go to get the inspiration to create a life they love. Pinterest shows users visual recommendations called Pins. Pins are created by both individuals and businesses by uploading photos or videos or bookmarking content from the web, with the option to provide a text caption. Users can save and organise these recommendations into collections called boards. Browsing and saving visual ideas on Pinterest helps users imagine what their future could look like, and go from inspiration to reality. Some of our most common use cases include fashion, food, beauty, home decor, and travel.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Introduction to Pinterest

paragraphs.text: Creating a Pin or saving a Pin to a board is not the same as making a post on a social media site. Pins are not broadcast to a user's network and the Pin is not highlighted through notifications or alerts. Users can choose to keep uploaded Pins private, or make them public with comments turned on or off.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Introduction to Pinterest

paragraphs.text: Pinterest has a longstanding commitment to making our platform a safe place for everyone, which is important for both our users and for our business goals. We view a positive, inspiring environment as essential to our user experience, and accordingly, we have developed Community Guidelines that broadly prohibit various types of content that may be harmful to users of any age. We have engaged with third-party expert groups when creating and updating our policies, and will continue to do so. These expert groups may flag risks and content trends related to different types of harm, as well as lessons learned from the wider content ecosystem.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Safety and wellbeing on our platform

paragraphs.text: We enforce our policies through a robust content moderation system, utilising a combination of automated tools, which use a combination of signals to identify and take action against potentially violating content; manual review, in which human reviewers moderate content identified by our automated tools or reported by users or third parties, as well as conduct targeted enforcement actions; and hybrid actions, in which a team member determines that a Pin violates policy and automated systems help expand that decision to enforce against machine-identified matching Pins.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Safety and wellbeing on our platform

evidence_quotes.quote: Pinterest offers a private profile as the default and only option for users under the age of 16, and the default option for users aged 16 and 17.
Private accounts are undiscoverable on Pinterest search and search engines

items.topic: Visibility Of Profiles/boards/Pins To Followers Only

evidence_quotes.quote: profiles, boards and Pins for these users will only be visible to followers approved by the user.
Teens under the age of 16 can only send and receive messages from mutual followers who they have invited to follow them through a unique profile link.
Teens under the age of 18 can only receive messages from mutual followers, and can only receive message requests from users they follow.

Parental Passcode feature for users under the age of 18, which allows parents and caregivers to set up a 4-digit passcode to lock certain settings and have oversight of their teen's Pinterest account.

paragraphs.text: Alongside global enforcement of our content policies, Pinterest utilises a number of features that are specifically designed to promote teen safety on our platform. For example, Pinterest offers a private profile as the default and only option for users under the age of 16, and the default option for users aged 16 and 17. Private accounts are undiscoverable on Pinterest search and search engines, and profiles, boards and Pins for these users will only be visible to followers approved by the user. Users with private profiles are able to connect with family and friends by sending a unique profile link, and all users have the ability to review and remove followers. Further, teens under the age of 16 can only send and receive messages from mutual followers who they have invited to follow them through a unique profile link. Teens under the age of 18 can only receive messages from mutual followers, and can only receive message requests from users they follow. Pinterest also offers a Parental Passcode feature for users under the age of 18, which allows parents and caregivers to set up a 4-digit passcode to lock certain settings and have oversight of their teen's Pinterest account.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Safety and wellbeing on our platform

paragraphs.text: In addition to leveraging our own internal tools, Pinterest engages with industry groups and external third-party experts to provide additional content moderation support. These third parties are experts in specific harm types, and they keep us informed of online trends and help us detect whether those trends are occurring on Pinterest. The new and emerging trends and other signals we receive from these experts are reviewed and, where appropriate, we build these trends and signals into our content moderation tools. We may also carry out targeted enforcement efforts based on signals provided by these third parties and update our moderation tools based on the results of those enforcement efforts.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Safety and wellbeing on our platform

paragraphs.text: In addition to moderating potentially harmful content, we take numerous proactive steps to support the wellbeing of the people who use our service. For example, Pinterest surfaces advisories in response to search terms for sensitive topics such as self harm or disordered eating, which connect users with free, country-specific support resources and helplines. We are also the first major online platform to publicly support phone-free school policies, and are currently testing an in-app test prompt in the United States, Canada, and United Kingdom encouraging teens to close our app during school hours and turn off all device notifications. Our Help Centre also contains several pages with guidance on how to combat harmful content on Pinterest along with links to external resources, including pages on suicide, self-harm, and domestic violence prevention; and on combating non-consensual intimate imagery.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Safety and wellbeing on our platform

evidence_quotes.quote: content personalisation
using AI to show representation of various body types, skin tones, and hair patterns in search results

paragraphs.text: Pinterest has also demonstrated a strong commitment to media literacy. We engage in various media literacy measures, including publishing detailed and readable policies; providing various articles on safety, privacy, content personalisation, and wellness throughout our Help Centre; surfacing search advisories in response to search queries regarding sensitive topics; publishing robust transparency reporting; using AI to show representation of various body types, skin tones, and hair patterns in search results; and promoting civility and respect online through our Creator Code, a set of guidelines prompting Pinterest users to lead with kindness when creating new content or interacting with other people on Pinterest, as well as a just-in-time reminder which surfaces the first time a user makes a comment, reminding them that comments should be kind, purposeful and constructive. We also recently launched our (Me)dia Mindfulness campaign to provide users with quality information, tools, and resources that focus on empowering users to engage, analyze, evaluate, create and act confidently - on Pinterest and across the digital world.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Safety and wellbeing on our platform

paragraphs.text: These efforts are effective in providing a high level of safety and wellbeing for people on Pinterest, but we know that the work needed to achieve our goal of providing a safe and positive platform for our users is ongoing. We're committed to continually reviewing and updating our policies, tools, and technologies based on evolving risks, and finding new ways to promote media literacy and user awareness of our safety features. Keeping our community safe is an industry-wide challenge as online trends and technological solutions continue to evolve, and we remain committed to exploring the best ways to achieve this goal. Indeed, as a founding signatory of The Inspired Internet Pledge,

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Safety and wellbeing on our platform

paragraphs.text: Pinterest has publicly pledged to centre its commitment to making the internet a healthier place through its product, thought leadership, policies, and strategic giving.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Safety and wellbeing on our platform

paragraphs.text: Accordingly, Pinterest is closely aligned with the DFA initiative’s goals of ensuring safety and empowerment for users and accountability for platforms, while safeguarding innovation and competition. As detailed herein, though, we have some concerns regarding the proportionality of certain potential measures and their harmonisation within the existing framework of digital regulation, particularly in the context of Pinterest’s service and existing safety measures. Pinterest appreciates the opportunity to respond to the call for evidence, and looks forward to continued engagement with the Commission on these topics.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Safety and wellbeing on our platform

paragraphs.text: Pinterest is a member of the Computer & Communications Industry Association (CCIA Europe) as well as Middle Tech Europe (MTE), and as such, we echo the points made in their submissions regarding the regulatory approach to be taken by the DFA initiative, as well as its place in the existing framework of digital regulation in Europe.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
General observations on the DFA initiative

paragraphs.text: In particular, these submissions detail how much of the DFA initiative’s objectives can be achieved through consistent and coordinated enforcement of existing consumer protection provisions, which already provide strong protection to users online. In order to provide legal clarity for platforms (as well as users) and avoid barriers to competition, any new measures enacted in the DFA should build on existing frameworks by clarifying and simplifying rules, rather than adding requirements that may be conflicting or duplicative.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
General observations on the DFA initiative

paragraphs.text: Further, these submissions illustrate how the DFA initiative should adopt a proportionate and evidence-based approach in light of the wide diversity of features, functionalities, risk profiles, existing mitigation measures, and operational capacities across online platforms. Rather than prescriptive, one-size-fits-all solutions, the DFA should grant companies the flexibility to meet its objectives in a way that suits their services, applying the measures that correlate to risks they actually face in a manner that is proportionate to the level of each risk. Such an approach will help preserve competition in the digital sector and allow platforms to implement measures that are suitable and effective for their own platforms, while avoiding any steps that are unnecessary to improve their users’ safety.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
General observations on the DFA initiative

paragraphs.text: We refer the Commission to the CCIA and MTE submissions for in-depth comments regarding proportionality and harmonisation of the DFA initiative with other EU regulations. In addition, this response highlights how these principles apply to the potential DFA measures that are most relevant to Pinterest's service.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
General observations on the DFA initiative

evidence_quotes.quote: providing users a mechanism to opt in or out of personalised advertising

paragraphs.text: The Commission's Public Consultation on the Digital Fairness Act (the "Consultation") identifies several potential measures related to personalised content, such as providing users a mechanism to opt in or out of personalised advertising, as well as restrictions on personalised advertising that targets users based on their age. Like many online platforms, personalised recommendations are a key component of the service Pinterest users rely on, and accordingly we believe the Commission's approach to measures related to personalisation should be evidence-based and designed to support transparency and user choice, while avoiding strict prohibitions on the features currently enjoyed by users. The DFA initiative should also strive to complement the extensive body of existing regulations in this area, to avoid regulatory overlap and legal uncertainty for service providers.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Restrictions on personalisation

evidence_quotes.quote: Personalisation is at the heart of the Pinterest experience because it ensures that every user sees content that is relevant and inspiring to them.

paragraphs.text: Personalisation is at the heart of the Pinterest experience because it ensures that every user sees content that is relevant and inspiring to them. On Pinterest, content isn't chronological, it's personal. With billions of Pins on countless topics, personalisation helps cut through the noise, surfacing the ideas and products that best match each individual's interests, tastes, and goals. Our users expect Pinterest to understand their unique style and preferences and provide a feed that feels tailor-made for them. By analyzing what Pinners search for, save, and engage with, Pinterest continuously fine-tunes the content each person sees, making it easier and more enjoyable for them to discover relevant, useful, and inspiring content.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Restrictions on personalisation

evidence_quotes.quote: Personalisation is an expected and highly valued component of the user experience.
Users also reported that they actively avoided using services that they perceived to be deceitful or manipulative.

paragraphs.text: Personalisation is an expected and highly valued component of the user experience. Indeed, according to a recent survey of European users conducted by the Nextrade Group, over 70 percent reported that personalised services were valuable and improved their online experience, and over 85 percent understand and accept that data collection is necessary for personalisation. Users also reported that they actively avoided using services that they perceived to be deceitful or manipulative.[7]

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Restrictions on personalisation

evidence_quotes.quote: the data that's used to provide recommendations.

paragraphs.text: Pinterest already offers users several methods to control the content they see, as well as the data that's used to provide recommendations. Users can use our Home Feed

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Restrictions on personalisation

paragraphs.text: [7] For more information on the survey, please see: https://www.nextradegroupllc.com/digital-fairness-act-survey

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Restrictions on personalisation

evidence_quotes.quote: Tuner to deemphasise recommendations on their Home Feed based on specific Pins they have viewed, interests they have selected, boards they have created, or users that they follow.

Pinterest also offers Privacy and Data Settings that let users adjust how Pinterest shares and uses certain information to personalise ads on Pinterest or to show users ads about Pinterest on other websites and apps.

paragraphs.text: Tuner to deemphasise recommendations on their Home Feed based on specific Pins they have viewed, interests they have selected, boards they have created, or users that they follow. They can also hide individual Pins from their home feed, which Pinterest treats as a signal to not recommend similar content. Pinterest also offers Privacy and Data Settings that let users adjust how Pinterest shares and uses certain information to personalise ads on Pinterest or to show users ads about Pinterest on other websites and apps. All of these options are easy to locate, easy to use, and can be accessed at any time.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Restrictions on personalisation

evidence_quotes.quote: DSA's ban on targeted advertising based on sensitive data categories

paragraphs.text: Personalisation is already heavily regulated in the EU through the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), ePrivacy Directive, Digital Services Act (DSA), Digital Markets Act (DMA), and AI Act. These laws provide strong safeguards regarding profiling, lawful bases for processing, use of sensitive data categories, user consent, and transparency, including specific provisions aimed at protecting minors, such as the DSA's ban on targeted advertising based on sensitive data categories, as well its prohibition on advertising to minors based on profiling.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Restrictions on personalisation

evidence_quotes.quote: personalisation is already heavily regulated

paragraphs.text: In light of the fact that personalisation is already heavily regulated, as well as the considerable benefits it provides to users, the DFA should only impose further restrictions on personalisation to the extent there are discernable harms to users as a result of identifiable gaps in the current legal framework. To the extent it does impose new measures, it should aim to do so in a way that preserves value to users and encourages competition among platforms that depend on personalised services. These goals could be accomplished by avoiding blanket prohibitions on service providers' offerings, and instead focusing on measures that support transparency and choice for users, with a flexible approach to implementation that accounts for each platform's unique set of features and use cases.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Restrictions on personalisation

evidence_quotes.quote: addictive design features
infinite scrolling, ephemeral stories, autoplay, penalties for disengagement, and recommender systems that are designed to increase user engagement.

paragraphs.text: The Consultation defines potentially "addictive design features" to include infinite scrolling, ephemeral stories, autoplay, penalties for disengagement, and recommender systems that are designed to increase user engagement. It sets out several possible approaches to regulating such features, including turning them off by default (either for minors or all users), prohibiting them completely for minors, allowing users to toggle these features on and off, and/or allowing users to choose the criteria for the recommendations they receive. Based on our experience, we believe that platforms should not be deemed "addictive" based solely on their feature set, and further that any new rules in this space should be complementary to existing requirements, applied in a

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Potentially addictive design features

paragraphs.text: risk-based and proportional manner, and avoid disruption to strongly-held consumer expectations.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Potentially addictive design features

paragraphs.text: Rather than being optimised for view time, Pinterest is focused on creating positive outcomes for users by helping them go from online inspiration to offline reality. People come to Pinterest looking to visualise their future, make plans, celebrate special moments, learn new skills, and take action, rather than simply liking other people's posts or scrolling through the latest news. This is reflected in Pinterest's most common use cases, which include food and drink, fashion, home decor, crafting, and do-it-yourself projects.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Potentially addictive design features

evidence_quotes.quote: We do this by tuning our recommender systems to prioritise explicit user signals – not just views alone.
filter results by skin tone, hair pattern, and body type.

paragraphs.text: Our goal is to provide a meaningful and productive experience that empowers our users to go from discovering a great idea online, to implementing that idea in their real life. In order to do so, we deliberately engineer our recommender systems to prioritise high-quality and inspirational content over content that is merely new or trending. We do this by tuning our recommender systems to prioritise explicit user signals – not just views alone. This helps users find what inspires them based on their engagement with Pinterest (i.e., recent searches, Pins they save or view, explicit signals such as selected topics of interest) rather than optimising for time spent on our platform or recommending content or actions based on who the individual follows. Pinterest also utilises non-engagement signals, such as in-app surveys (where users can tell us about their experience on the platform) or independent assessments of content quality, which are usually generated by manual labelling. These nonengagement signals provide a balance to engagement signals in our content ranking and also help us put our values into action, for example by offering inclusive fashion and beauty search technology that allows users to filter results by skin tone, hair pattern, and body type.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Potentially addictive design features

evidence_quotes.quote: fine-tune the recommendations they receive.
tune their home feed to customise their preferences
including by limiting specific topics of interest

paragraphs.text: As detailed above, our users also have the ability to fine-tune the recommendations they receive. Pinners can tune their home feed to customise their preferences, including by limiting specific topics of interest, hiding individual Pins from their home feed, or unfollowing the board, topic or account that the Pin came from.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Potentially addictive design features

evidence_quotes.quote: our recommender system is highly personalised
downrank content similar to content that has been deactivated by our content moderation systems

paragraphs.text: As well as surfacing positive content, we also tune our machine learning models to de-emphasise low quality content by utilising machine learning models that downrank content similar to content that has been deactivated by our content moderation systems, as well as content that is not policy-violating but may be harmful in large amounts. In addition, we do not encourage platform engagement through social features, and since our recommender system is highly personalised, Pinterest is not prone to disseminating "viral" content.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Potentially addictive design features

evidence_quotes.quote: the mere presence of the types of features identified in the Consultation as potentially "addictive" does not equal an addictive user experience.

paragraphs.text: Building our algorithmic systems to prioritise high quality, inspirational, and useful content enables us to create a more positive environment that can support the mental health of our users. We believe that Pinterest demonstrates that the mere presence of the types of features identified in the Consultation as potentially "addictive" does not equal an addictive user experience. Instead, the user experience is defined by myriad platform-specific variables, such as recommender system design, safety features, and common use cases.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Potentially addictive design features

evidence_quotes.quote: DSA's prohibition on "dark patterns"
transparency requirements for recommender system parameters
obligations for Very Large Online Platforms (VLOPs) to mitigate systemic risks

paragraphs.text: Similar to personalisation, there are also a number of existing regulations in this space, particularly the DSA's prohibition on "dark patterns", transparency requirements for recommender system parameters, and obligations for Very Large Online Platforms (VLOPs) to mitigate systemic risks. Accordingly, any additional regulation in this area should be pursued only where there is an evidence-based need, and in a manner that complements existing frameworks in order to reduce legal uncertainty and compliance burden on platforms. In addition, application of new rules to individual providers should be proportionate and risk-based, accounting for the level of risk posed by the service.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Potentially addictive design features

evidence_quotes.quote: continuous scrolling and autoplay, are very well-established elements of the user experience, to the point where consumers expect them from online services.

paragraphs.text: It should further be noted that certain features identified in the Consultation, such as continuous scrolling and autoplay, are very well-established elements of the user experience, to the point where consumers expect them from online services. As such, regulation in this area should be aimed at user empowerment, rather than prohibition of features that users find desirable.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Potentially addictive design features

evidence_quotes.quote: requirements for clear and prominent disclosure of advertising content

paragraphs.text: The Consultation identifies several potential measures to curb problematic commercial practices by social media influencers, such as hidden marketing and the promotion and sale of potentially harmful products. These include requirements for clear and prominent disclosure of advertising content, measures to be taken by brands and agencies to hold influencers accountable to legal obligations, and restrictions on specific types of marketing claims that may be harmful to minors. As this is an area that is heavily regulated through existing consumer protection laws as well as industry standards, we believe that users would be best served by consistent and coordinated enforcement of existing consumer protection provisions, as well as consumer education and empowerment measures.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Influencer marketing

evidence_quotes.quote: require all content posted in connection with paid partnerships to disclose that it is commercial in nature,

paragraphs.text: Pinterest is solely monetized through the sale of advertising space, but we do permit users to post sponsored or branded content. Like many platforms, we have policies that require all content posted in connection with paid partnerships to disclose that it is commercial in nature, which may be done using a paid partnership tool offered by

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Influencer marketing

paragraphs.text: PDF

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Influencer marketing

evidence_quotes.quote: disclosure in the Pin's image or description.
paid partnerships

paragraphs.text: Pinterest or by making a disclosure in the Pin's image or description. We also require all paid partnerships to adhere to our Advertising Guidelines, which prohibit and restrict certain types of advertising content and targeting, as well as our spam policy. Users can report content that they suspect has not been properly labeled for review and potential enforcement by our Trust & Safety team. For further context, it should be noted that these are steps Pinterest has taken proactively. We are not aware of having received any complaints from users or brands concerning suspected hidden advertisements or other misconduct by influencers on Pinterest.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Influencer marketing

evidence_quotes.quote: Ireland's Online Safety Code imposes obligations specifically related to online influencers.

paragraphs.text: Along with requirements such as these that are common across platforms, creators and partner brands are subject to general consumer protection law as well as standards set by advertising industry organisations. In addition, Ireland's Online Safety Code imposes obligations specifically related to online influencers. As this is an area where strong legislative safeguards for consumers are already in place, we believe it would be preferable to focus on enforcement of existing rules as well as promoting consumer education measures, rather than additional regulatory requirements that may be redundant and add to platforms' regulatory burden without commensurate user benefit.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Influencer marketing

paragraphs.text: Pinterest is grateful for the opportunity to contribute to the important work being done by the Commission in obtaining feedback to analyse the potential impact of the DFA. We welcome further engagement as part of this process, and if it would be of assistance, we would be happy to elaborate on or discuss any of the points raised in this response or any other matters of interest to the Commission.

section_path: Pinterest Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence: Digital Fairness Act
Conclusion

paragraphs.text: The contribution of the Central European Lawyers Initiative (CELI) is provided in the attached document.
